channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Advice for 2026 commencement speakers: Don't bring up AI
item.description: Commencement speakers who bring up the sweeping changes that artificial intelligence is driving are facing boos from the Class of 2026.
item.content:encoded:
Commencement speakers who bring up the sweeping changes that artificial intelligence is driving are facing boos from the Class of 2026.

(Image credit: University of Central Florida and Middle Tennessee State University via Storyful)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO plans reveal blockbuster spending on rockets and AI
item.description: The company is on track to pull off the largest IPO in history — making CEO Elon Musk even wealthier.
item.content:encoded:
The company is on track to pull off the largest IPO in history — making CEO Elon Musk even wealthier.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: Meta slashes 8,000 jobs as it pivots towards AI
item.description: Facebook and Instagram's parent company has been investing huge sums of money in AI, but it lags behind competitors.
item.content:encoded:
Facebook and Instagram's parent company has been investing huge sums of money in AI, but it lags behind competitors.

(Image credit: Jeff Chiu)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Minnesota becomes first state to ban prediction markets
item.description: While dozens of states have taken legal action against the controversial industry, Minnesota is the first state to pass a law making it a felony for companies like Kalshi and Polymarket to operate.
item.content:encoded:
While dozens of states have taken legal action against the controversial industry, Minnesota is the first state to pass a law making it a felony for companies like Kalshi and Polymarket to operate.

(Image credit: Steve Karnowski)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: What we know about how the U.S. government uses spyware (and what we don't)
item.description: Critics of spyware, which can be used to remotely hack into phones, worry the Trump administration is eroding policies that stigmatized the commercial spyware industry.
item.content:encoded:
Critics of spyware, which can be used to remotely hack into phones, worry the Trump administration is eroding policies that stigmatized the commercial spyware industry.

(Image credit: Menahem Kahana)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Jury dismisses all claims in Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman
item.description: A jury in a California courtroom has dismissed all claims in the case Elon Musk brought against Sam Altman and OpenAI.
item.content:encoded: A jury in a California courtroom has dismissed all claims in the case Elon Musk brought against Sam Altman and OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Rachael Myrow

item.title: Jury dismisses all claims in Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman
item.description: Musk had sought to oust Altman from his leadership position over claims that he and others breached their duty to OpenAI's original nonprofit mission and unjustly enriched themselves.
item.content:encoded:
Musk had sought to oust Altman from his leadership position over claims that he and others breached their duty to OpenAI's original nonprofit mission and unjustly enriched themselves.

(Image credit: Godofredo A. Vásquez)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: How Trump may be changing his stance on AI regulation
item.description: The Trump administration's approach on AI so far has been to prioritize innovation over regulation. But recently, there's been a shift in rhetoric.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration's approach on AI so far has been to prioritize innovation over regulation. But recently, there's been a shift in rhetoric.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman takes the stand to fend off Elon Musk's accusations he 'stole a charity'
item.description: The trial pits two of tech's most famous personalities against each other and could lead to seismic changes for the maker of ChatGPT.
item.content:encoded:
The trial pits two of tech's most famous personalities against each other and could lead to seismic changes for the maker of ChatGPT.

(Image credit: JOSH EDELSON)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: She spent a year using AI to do almost everything. Here's what she learned
item.description: Tech writer Joanna Stern used AI to read medical results, respond to texts and serve as her therapist. She says her emotional connection to it was unsettling. Her new book is I Am Not a Robot.
item.content:encoded: Tech writer Joanna Stern used AI to read medical results, respond to texts and serve as her therapist. She says her emotional connection to it was unsettling. Her new book is I Am Not a Robot.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross
